nativeRange.habitat: Shaded margins of streams, rivers, and forest swamps in tropical West and Central Africa; grows epiphytically on wood and rocks, often partially or fully submerged, and tolerates emersed conditions

decorPreferences: shaded placement to reduce leaf algae

goodWith: most African cichlids (tough leaves resist nibbling)

summary: A medium-to-large West and Central African rhizome plant with long, lance-shaped leaves. Generally considered beginner-friendly; the rhizome must be attached to hardscape and never buried.
careNotes: Anubias heterophylla is one of the tallest and most architectural species in the Anubias genus, distinguished by its long, narrow, lance-shaped leaves. It is widely traded under the synonym 'Anubias Congensis' and the trade name 'Anubias Lanceolata', though all three names refer to the same biological species. Mature plants typically reach 25–50 cm tall, with individual leaves sometimes growing 20–30 cm long under good conditions, making it best suited to midground or background placement in medium and larger tanks. Like all Anubias, the rhizome (the thick horizontal stem from which leaves and roots emerge) MUST NOT BE BURIED in substrate — burial causes the rhizome to rot and the plant to die. Instead, attach the plant to driftwood, rocks, or other hardscape using cotton thread, fishing line, or a small dab of cyanoacrylate (super) glue; the roots eventually anchor on their own. Husbandry is otherwise straightforward and forgiving: low to medium lighting is preferred (intense light combined with slow growth promotes leaf algae), and CO2 injection is optional rather than required — it accelerates growth somewhat but is not necessary for health. Growth is slow regardless of conditions. The plant feeds primarily from the water column, so liquid fertilization is more impactful than substrate fertilization. Temperature range based on cross-source consensus (Modern Aquarium 20–28 °C, aqua-fish.net and Aquarium Plants Factory 20–30 °C); 22–28 °C represents the comfortable mid-range. pH tolerance is broad (5.6–8.0 reported); 6.0–7.8 is a safer practical range. Trade-name note: The 'Anubias Lanceolata' marketing name is sometimes used for both true A. heterophylla and a narrower-leaved A. heterophylla var. crispa cultivar; care is identical.
breedingNotes: Propagated by rhizome division: with a clean sharp blade, cut a healthy rhizome into sections, ensuring each section retains at least 3 healthy leaves and some attached roots. Re-attach each section to hardscape — do not bury. Like other Anubias, the plant can flower underwater, producing a small spathe-and-spadix inflorescence typical of the Araceae family, but seed propagation is not practical in the home aquarium.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Tropica search page for Anubias heterophylla returned navigation-only content (no product detail page rendered to the fetcher). Fell back to walk down the secondary source list per playbook; documented swap below.

additional.name: Modern Aquarium
additional.notes: Designated secondary; used as effective primary after Tropica search did not render a product page. Confirmed native West African range, slow growth, low–moderate lighting, CO2 not required, easy care difficulty, lance-shaped leaves, midground/background placement, and the rhizome-must-not-be-buried instruction. Stated temperature 68–82 °F (20–28 °C).

additional.name: Aqua-Fish.net
additional.notes: Cross-checked: family Araceae, native range (Cameroon, Guinea, Angola, DR Congo), size 25–60 cm, temperature 20–30 °C, pH 5.6–8.0, hardness 0–18 dGH, slow growth, low-to-moderate light preference, gravel substrate, rhizome-division propagation, easy difficulty.

additional.name: Aquarium Plants Factory
additional.notes: Cross-checked: native range (Cameroon, Guinea, Angola, Zaire / DR Congo), temperature 68–86 °F (20–30 °C), pH 5.6–8.0, low–medium light, CO2 not required, slow growth, background/midground placement, easy difficulty, rhizome-burial warning. Leaf length up to 12 inches reported.

additional.name: Buce Plant
additional.notes: Cross-checked: trade name 'Congo Anubias', pH 6.0–7.5, low light, CO2 not necessary (promotes faster growth), slow-to-moderate growth, midground-to-background placement, propagation by rhizome separation, rhizome-must-stay-above-substrate warning. Confirms Anubias heterophylla and Anubias congensis are the same plant.
